title: Collecting a Receivable
event: A client pays Tran Consulting the $1,200 it owed from work completed last month. Prepare the entry.

lines.label: collection
lines.trap: Tempting to pair this debit with revenue — resist. Look at what already sits in AR.

lines.label: claim settled
lines.why: The claim is settled — the receivable comes off the books.
lines.trap: NO revenue in this entry. Revenue was recorded last month when the work was done; recording it again double-counts.

memorize.body: Collecting a receivable is an asset SWAP (AR → Cash), not an earning event. Revenue only ever gets recorded once.
Total assets don't change here — one asset up, another down by the same amount.

questions.prompt: How much revenue does Tran recognize when collecting the $1,200?

distractors.feedback: The revenue already happened — last month, when the work was performed. This entry just converts the receivable into cash.

build.scaffold: Two assets: one up, one down. If you typed 'Revenue,' back up a month — it's already there.
